messages.content:
You are a senior equity risk rater evaluating a significant market event.

Your job is to state the primary risk implication — not just describe what happened, but explain what it means for a holder of this position. If the event has both positive and negative angles, resolve which effect is primary and why.

Return strict JSON:
{
  "analysis_text": "3-6 sentence analysis that leads with the primary implication, resolves competing forces, and states what would confirm or negate the risk",
  "impact_horizon": "immediate|near_term|long_term",
  "risk_direction": "improving|neutral|worsening",
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "scenario_summary": "one sentence stating the primary risk implication",
  "key_implications": ["...", "..."],
  "followup_notes": ["...", "..."]
}

For each event below, analyze the primary risk implication for a holder — not just what happened, but what it means. If an event has competing positive and negative angles, resolve which is primary.

If evidence quality is title_only or headline_summary, do not act as if you read a full article. Lower confidence and describe the rating as limited by available data.

Events:
[0] Evidence quality: full_body
    Title: A. O. Smith Corporation (NYSE:AOS) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey
    Summary: A. O. Smith Corporation (NYSE:AOS) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript Insider Monkey
    Body: ')}.icon-sharing-widget-x{background-image:url('data:image/svg+xml, twitter (1) ')}.icon-sharing-widget-linkedin{background-image:url('data:image/svg+xml, linkedin ')}.icon-sharing-widget-mail{background-image:url('data:image/svg+xml, email (2) ')}.icon-ai{background-image:url('data:image/svg+xml, ai ')}#shadow{zoom:1;-webkit-opacity:.85;-moz-opacity:.85;opacity:.85;opacity:.85;background:#121212;position:fixed;left:0;top:0;width:100%;height:100%;z-index:1000;display:none}#shadow.search-shadow{top:70px}@media (max-width:1024px){#shadow.search-shadow{top:64px}}.overlay{position:fixed;width:500px;border:1px solid #dadada;background:#fff;top:80px;left:50%;margin-left:-250px;padding:24px;display:none;-webkit-box-shadow:3px 3px 3px #777;-moz-box-shadow:3px 3px 3px #777;box-shadow:3px 3px 3px #777;z-index:1100}@media screen and (max-width:768px){.overlay{width:100%;margin-left:0;top:0;left:0;h

Return a JSON array with one object per event in order.
Each object has: analysis_text, impact_horizon, risk_direction, confidence, scenario_summary, key_implications, followup_notes.

response:
```json
[
  {
    "analysis_text": "The provided evidence is severely limited — the body content consists entirely of CSS/HTML styling code with no actual earnings call transcript content. Without the substantive financial results, management commentary, or guidance from A. O. Smith's Q1 2026 call, a meaningful risk analysis cannot be performed. The rating is constrained to a structural observation: as a water heater and water treatment manufacturer, A. O. Smith faces exposure to housing market cycles, commodity inflation (particularly steel and copper), and consumer discretionary spending. Any guidance revision, margin commentary, or demand signals that would inform a forward risk assessment are absent from this data.",
    "impact_horizon": "near_term",
    "risk_direction": "neutral",
    "confidence": 0.15,
    "scenario_summary": "Insufficient transcript content prevents identification of primary risk signals; the rating is constrained by data quality despite the 'full_body' designation.",
    "key_implications": [
      "Cannot assess Q1 2026 actual results vs. expectations",
      "Cannot evaluate management guidance or forward outlook",
      "Cannot identify margin pressure, demand trends, or inventory adjustments",
      "Rating is analytically non-actionable without transcript content"
    ],
    "followup_notes": [
      "Re-obtain transcript body with actual Q1 2026 earnings call content including revenue, EPS, and guidance",
      "Focus on any commentary regarding housing starts, consumer replacement demand, or commodity cost pass-through",
      "A. O. Smith's business is moderately cyclical (tied to new construction and home improvement); tariff or trade policy changes would be a key risk if mentioned on the call"
    ]
  }
]
```
